notes: AI-facing tile legend for the scribble tileset (128×128 hand-drawn art over a 14-column × 11-row sheet, 154 tiles total). Generate a map as a Tiled tile layer: an object with "type": "tilelayer", a "name", integer "width" and "height", and a flat "data" array of length width*height. Each entry in "data" is the GID of a tile defined below (use the id shown as each tile's key, e.g. 8 for grass). GIDs assume the tileset is referenced at firstgid: 1 (the only configuration this codebase uses); GID = row*14 + col + 1 over the 14×11 tilesheet. The array is row-major: the first "width" entries are the top row, left to right, then the next row. Only use ids listed below; do not invent ids. TRANSPARENT TWINS: columns 8-13 (GIDs in the gaps 9-14, 23-28, 37-42, 51-56, 65-70, 79-84, 93-98, 107-112) are transparent-background copies of the tiles in columns 1-6 of the same row (shifted +7 columns; e.g. the tile at col 8 is the transparent version of the tile at col 1). Their names end in "_transparent". They are "layer": "object" overlays with no floor of their own — place them on top of a ground tile (e.g. a transparent wall over grass). Passability is copied from their floored twin. ROTATION / FLIP: most directional tiles (wall edges, corners, bridges, paths) ship only in one orientation; to render them at other angles, OR the GID with one of Tiled's standard flip flags: 0x80000000 (flip H), 0x40000000 (flip V), 0x20000000 (anti-diagonal flip, used for 90° rotations). Common combinations: rotate 90° CW = 0xA0000000 (+2684354560); rotate 180° = 0xC0000000 (+3221225472); rotate 270° CW = 0x60000000 (+1610612736). Maps may have two stacked tile layers: a "ground" layer (one of the "layer": "ground" tiles below per cell) and an optional "object" layer (one of the "layer": "object" tiles per cell, or 0 for empty) drawn on top. "passable" is authoritative for reachability/validation — a cell is passable iff both its ground tile and its object tile (if any) are passable. Impassable terrain (chasm) can be crossed by placing a bridge or walkway tile over it.

1.name: blank
1.description: Featureless light tile with no surface detail. Passable. Use sparingly — for default walkable floors prefer stone_floor (GID 15) or its variants.

2.name: path_corner_se
2.description: Dirt path corner; the path enters from the bottom edge and turns to exit on the right.

3.name: stone_wall_corner_tl
3.description: Solid stone wall corner piece (top-left). Blocks movement and sight.

4.name: stone_wall_top
4.description: Solid stone wall segment placed along the top of the tile. Blocks movement and sight.

5.name: doorway_top
5.description: An open doorway (no door) set in the top part of the tile. Passable opening; place over a wall tile.

6.name: crate
6.description: A wooden crate marked with an X. Blocks movement; searchable for loot.

7.name: wooden_plank
7.description: Light wooden plank ground — a passable wooden surface. Use for tavern floors, dock boards, raised wooden platforms, and similar walkable wood. Pair with wood_floor (85) for variation.

8.name: grass
8.description: Solid medium-green grass. Default outdoor ground fill.

9.name: path_corner_se_transparent
9.description: Dirt path corner; the path enters from the bottom edge and turns to exit on the right. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of path_corner_se.

10.name: stone_wall_corner_tl_transparent
10.description: Solid stone wall corner piece (top-left). Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of stone_wall_corner_tl.

11.name: stone_wall_top_transparent
11.description: Solid stone wall segment placed along the top of the tile. Blocks movement and sight. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of stone_wall_top.

12.name: doorway_top_transparent
12.description: An open doorway (no door) set in the top part of the tile. Passable opening; place over a wall tile. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of doorway_top.

13.name: crate_transparent
13.description: A wooden crate marked with an X. Blocks movement; searchable for loot. Transparent background (no floor); overlay on top of a ground tile. Twin of crate.

14.name: wooden_plank_transparent
14.description: Light wooden plank overlay — a passable wooden surface drawn on top of an existing ground tile. Transparent background. Use to add wooden detail (boards, planking, decking) over a non-wood ground without replacing it. Twin of wooden_plank.

15.name: stone_floor
15.description: Pale grey flagstone floor with faint grid lines. **Default walkable dungeon / interior floor** — pair with stone_floor_cracked (71) for variation in worn or ruined areas.

16.name: path_straight_v
16.description: Straight vertical dirt path running from the bottom edge to the top edge.

17.name: stone_wall_corner_curved
17.description: Curved stone wall corner piece. Blocks movement and sight.

18.name: half_wall_ul
18.description: A low half-wall in the upper-left of the tile. Passable; provides cover but does not block line of sight.

19.name: doorway_open_top
19.description: An open doorway with no door, set in the upper part of the tile. Passable opening.

20.name: brazier
20.description: A standing lit brazier. Blocks movement; emits light.

21.name: chair_n
21.description: A wooden chair, centered, facing north. Passable seat a character can occupy.

22.name: crate_closed
22.description: A closed crate with a slatted lid. Blocks movement; searchable for loot.

65534.name: void
65534.description: Pure black void / abyss. The renderer draws a solid black rectangle here (no sprite is sampled from the tileset) and the cell is impassable. Use this as the chasm fill — fill the impassable region with GID 65534 and lay bridge tiles (78 or 106) across it for crossings.
